ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON OCT-06-2011 IN 503, DEFENDANT THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATED GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1332(C)(6). THE TRIAL DATE OF OCTOBER 11, 2011 IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 28, 2011 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE CASE IS SEVERED FROM BRAYTON GROUP 579 AND ADDED TO BRAYTON GROUP 585. DISCOVERY IN THIS MATTER IS TO REMAIN CLOSED, WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE DEPOSITION OF WILLIAM SLAUGHTERBACK AND WITNESS GREG JEFFRESS AND EXPERT DISCOVERY WHICH WILL TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert M. Channel, Esq., of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party Thomas Dee Engineering Company
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Patrick Leaderich, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Company's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that: 1) John Leaderich was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant; 2) defendant manufactured, sold or supplied a defective product that Mr. Leaderich was exposed to; 3) defendant made an actionable false representation; and/or 4) defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Defendant's faillure to demonstrate that plaintiffs responded to written contention discovery precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542 = (220/HEK)
